scenario: Lobsters work session - audit the Story contract before a teardown rework

map_quality.rationale: The answer does not orient the agent at all: it never names Lobsters, never describes the Rails app layout (app/models, app/controllers, etc.), and never locates the Story model. The entire answer addresses vote-score recalculation mechanics, a completely different question.

specificity.rationale: A handful of file paths appear incidentally (app/models/story.rb, app/models/vote.rb, app/models/comment.rb) but they are cited to explain SQL score columns, not to map the project structure, so they provide negligible orientation value.

justification.rationale: No explanation is given for why the architecture is shaped the way it is, or why Story is the central submission model; the answer is silent on all architectural reasoning.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags no uncertainty about how layers connect because it never attempts to describe layers or connections in the orientation sense.

relationship.rationale: The only incidental relationship detail is that Vote#target is polymorphic and resolves to Comment or Story (vote.rb:185-191), but this is not framed as an orientation association and nothing about taggings, flags, or concerns is mentioned.

steps.step: Map the Story contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps only Story#update_score_and_recalculate! (story.rb:693-718) and calculated_hotness (story.rb:550); it never touches the actual contract: no Token concern, no has_many :comments/:votes/:taggings declarations, no broader model surface. An agent still needs to open story.rb from scratch to learn the contract.

specificity.rationale: The scoring-method citations are precise (story.rb:693-718, story.rb:550, vote.rb:181), but the contract pieces the rubric names — the Token concern file and the association declarations — are entirely absent, so specificity covers only a narrow slice of what was asked.

justification.rationale: The answer explains the race-condition caveat in the SQL aggregation but gives no explanation of why the Story contract is shaped as it is (Story as the central submission other records hang off). The WHY for the contract structure is missing.

uncertainty.rationale: The documented race condition (story.rb:691-692) is flagged, but the answer never acknowledges that it may have missed the concern or associations — there is no signal that contract coverage was incomplete.

relationship.rationale: Only the polymorphic Vote→target→Story dispatch path is stated; the rubric asks for the RELATION by which each contract piece binds to Story (which concern supplies what, which associations hang off), and none of those relationships appear.

steps.step: Trace how a story is created and ranked

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps vote-driven score recalculation (Vote#vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because, update_score_and_recalculate!), not the creation path: there is no stories controller create action, no URL/link handling, no markdown rendering layer, and no ordering of those layers. An agent trying to follow submission → storage would need to start exploration from scratch.

specificity.rationale: All cited file:line references are concrete, but they are for the wrong topic: none of the rubric-specified pieces (stories controller create, URL/link handler, markdowner) appear; only calculated_hotness at story.rb:550 is adjacent to the asked-for score machinery.

justification.rationale: The answer explains the race condition caveat and the flagger-commented exemption in the voting path, but provides no explanation for why the creation pipeline (URL handling before markdown, hotness at save time) is structured the way it is, which is what the rubric asks for.

uncertainty.rationale: The race condition is flagged, but the answer expresses no uncertainty about the creation path it never attempted to trace, leaving the agent with false confidence that nothing was missed.

relationship.rationale: The answer assembles an explicit chain for vote processing (call site → target resolution → polymorphic dispatch → SQL update), but the asked-for chain — submission → controller → URL/markdown → score — is entirely absent.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Story contract

map_quality.rationale: The answer narrows entirely to the vote/score-recalculation path and never produces the grouped-by-area audit the step demands; search (search.rb), stats/graph cache, inbound email (inbox_mailbox.rb), moderation controllers, markdowner, and content/rendering dependents are all absent, forcing the agent to do a complete fresh codebase sweep.

specificity.rationale: The slice it does cover (story.rb:693-718, vote.rb:181, comment.rb:339-363) is cited with precise file:line, but that slice is only one narrow area of the required exhaustive list, so specificity is present only within a domain that is itself wrong.

justification.rationale: It explains why votes drive hotness/confidence recalculation, but offers no justification for WHY the broader set of dependents (search indexer, mailbox, stats, moderation) is affected by a Story contract change, which is what the step asks.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer presents itself as 'All steps complete' despite missing at least five of the six required dependency areas; it flags no gaps, leaving the agent no signal that a major portion of the audit is unfinished.

relationship.rationale: Within the vote domain it does state connecting relationships (Vote#target resolves to Story via story_id, hidden_story.rb calls update_score_and_recalculate! directly), but the non-obvious dependents (search via indexer, mailbox via association, markdowner via rendering) that the step specifically calls out are entirely absent, so the bulk of the relationship map is missing.

map_quality.rationale: The answer is entirely about vote score/flag recalculation mechanics (Vote#vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because, update_score_and_recalculate!, calculated_hotness) and says nothing about presence or visibility guards in search indexing, stats, inbound email, or moderation paths — the four areas the step requires.

justification.rationale: The answer explains why score/hotness is recomputed, not why dependent paths must re-confirm a story is real and visible before acting — the question the step poses.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps only the voting/score-recalculation machinery (story.rb:693-718, comment.rb:339-363, vote.rb:181) and ignores rendering, search, stats, inbound email, and moderation entirely — the five areas the rubric requires. The output is a change map for update_score_and_recalculate!, not a blast radius across every dependent area, and provides no risk ranking.

specificity.rationale: Within the voting domain the file:line references are concrete and actionable (vote.rb:181, story.rb:693-718, comment.rb:360, hidden_story.rb:15/21, spec files named by line range), but the covered surface is so narrow that the specificity cannot compensate for what is absent.

justification.rationale: The answer explains WHY within its narrow scope (race condition documented at story.rb:691-692, flagger-commented exemption at story.rb:703-713, confidence_order byte packing) but offers no cross-area risk ranking or explanation of why rendering/search/email/moderation rank lower or higher than voting.

relationship.rationale: The polymorphic dispatch path (Vote#target → Story or Comment, vote.rb:185-191) and the hidden_story relationship (hidden_story.rb:15/21 calling update_score_and_recalculate!) are stated, but all relationships connecting the Story contract to search, stats, email, and moderation are absent.

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps the vote-scoring sub-system (vote.rb→story.rb:693-718, comment.rb:339-363, hidden_story.rb) with specs in fine detail, but omits entire areas the rubric requires: rendering/markdowner, search indexer, stats/graph cache, inbound-email mailbox, and moderation controllers — so the agent cannot land the Story contract change from this map alone without re-exploring those areas from scratch.
